epics.title: Remove obsolete tools and modernize the shared GNB

tasks.title: Remove Voicings and Music Fonts route segments
acceptance_criteria: The App Router no longer contains public pages for /voicings or /music-fonts.
Route-local components and styles used only by those pages are removed.
Shared music utilities and the remaining Canvas and Songs routes are preserved.
Previously removed /quiz and /chords routes remain absent.
done_definition: The production route manifest omits /voicings and /music-fonts.

tasks.title: Redesign the GNB for two remaining destinations
acceptance_criteria: The GNB presents a clear brand/home anchor and immediate Canvas and Songs access without pill-chip styling.
The active route is communicated by aria-current, text hierarchy, and a restrained non-pill visual indicator.
Mobile navigation fits 390px in a single compact header row with no horizontal overflow.
Every navigation target is at least 44px high and remains keyboard accessible.
The design uses the repository design-rule-engine, product-craft-workbench profile, Geist typography, semantic Tailwind/shadcn tokens, and relevant Vercel guidance.
done_definition: src/app/app-nav.tsx renders the approved responsive GNB on Canvas and Songs.

tasks.title: Verify routes and responsive navigation
acceptance_criteria: Browser before/after evidence covers desktop and 390px mobile layouts.
Browser checks confirm exact remaining navigation labels, active states, target sizes, header height, redundant-label absence, and available-width fit.
Direct requests to /voicings and /music-fonts return the normal not-found response.
Source search, production route manifest, lint, build, React review, and AI run validation pass.
Served styling is verified through computed browser values because no new reusable semantic CSS class is planned.
done_definition: verify.json records deterministic and screenshot-backed evidence with no blocking issue.

assumptions: The user wants Voicings and Score Lab removed entirely, not merely hidden from navigation.
With only two primary destinations, persistent direct links are more mobile-friendly than a menu button and hidden sheet.
The existing white product surface and Geist font remain appropriate; this task modernizes composition rather than changing the whole theme.
risks: Deleting route-local files must not remove similarly named Canvas components that are separate implementations.
A compact header can become cramped if brand text and navigation are not tested at 390px.
Old bookmarks to removed routes will receive the standard not-found page.
